en: The words of Agur son of Jakeh. The oracle. The man declares, I am weary, O God; I am weary, O God, and worn out.
zh: 雅基的兒子亞古珥的話，是 神的默示。 這人對以鐵，對以鐵和烏甲宣講：

en: Surely I am too stupid to be a man. I have not the understanding of a man.
zh: “我比眾人更愚頑， 也沒有聰明。

en: I have not learned wisdom, nor have I knowledge of the Holy One.
zh: 我沒有學習智慧， 也不曉得有關至聖者的知識。

en: Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son's name? Surely you know!
zh: 誰升上天，又降下來呢？ 誰收聚風在掌中呢？ 誰包水在衣服裡呢？ 誰立定地的四極呢？ 他的名字叫甚麼？他兒子的名字叫甚麼？ 你知道嗎？

en: Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
zh: 神所說的話句句都是煉淨的， 投靠他的，他就作他們的盾牌。

en: Do not add to his words, lest he rebuke you and you be found a liar.
zh: 他的話語，你不可增添； 恐怕他責備你，你就表明是說謊的。

en: Two things I ask of you; deny them not to me before I die:
zh: 我有兩件事求你， 在我死去以前，求你答應我。

en: Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that is needful for me,
zh: 求你使虛假和謊言遠離我； 不要使我貧窮，也不要使我富裕， 只要給我需用的食物。

en: lest I be full and deny you and say, "Who is the LORD?" or lest I be poor and steal and profane the name of my God.
zh: 免得我吃飽了，就不認你，說： ‘耶和華是誰？’ 又恐怕我貧窮，就偷竊， 污瀆了我 神的名。

en: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he curse you and you be held guilty.
zh: “不要向主人誹謗他的僕人， 恐怕他咒詛你，你就判為有罪。

en: There are those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
zh: 有一種人 咒詛父親， 又不給母親祝福。

en: There are those who are clean in their own eyes but are not washed of their filth.
zh: 有一種人自以為清潔， 卻沒有洗去自己的污穢。

en: There are those- how lofty are their eyes, how high their eyelids lift!
zh: 有一種人眼目多麼高傲， 他們的眼睛，長在頂上。

en: There are those whose teeth are swords, whose fangs are knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, the needy from among mankind.
zh: 有一種人牙如劍， 齒如刀， 要吞滅地上的困苦人 和世人中的貧窮人。

en: The leech has two daughters; "Give" and "Give," they cry. Three things are never satisfied; four never say, "Enough":
zh: 水蛭有兩個女兒，常說：‘給我！給我！’ 不知足的東西有三樣， 連總不說‘夠了’的共有四樣，

en: Sheol, the barren womb, the land never satisfied with water, and the fire that never says, "Enough."
zh: 就是陰間、不能生育的婦人、 水源不足之地 和總不說‘夠了’的火。

en: The eye that mocks a father and scorns to obey a mother will be picked out by the ravens of the valley and eaten by the vultures.
zh: 嘲笑父親， 藐視年老母親的， 他的眼必被谷中的烏鴉啄出來， 給雛鷹所吃。

en: Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand:
zh: 我測不透的奇事有三樣， 連我不知道的，共有四樣：

en: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a virgin.
zh: 就是鷹在空中飛翔之道， 蛇在磐石上爬行之道， 船在海中航行之道， 男與女交合之道。

en: This is the way of an adulteress: she eats and wipes her mouth and says, "I have done no wrong."
zh: 淫婦的行徑是這樣的： 她吃了，把嘴一抹， 就說：‘我沒有作錯事。’

en: Under three things the earth trembles; under four it cannot bear up:
zh: 使地震動的事有三樣， 連地也擔當不起的，共有四樣：

en: a slave when he becomes king, and a fool when he is filled with food;
zh: 就是奴僕作王， 愚頑人吃得飽，

en: an unloved woman when she gets a husband, and a maidservant when she displaces her mistress.
zh: 被人厭惡的女子出嫁， 婢女接替主母的地位。

en: Four things on earth are small, but they are exceedingly wise:
zh: 地上有四樣小東西， 非常聰明：

en: the ants are a people not strong, yet they provide their food in the summer;
zh: 螞蟻是微小的昆蟲， 卻能在夏天預備糧食。

en: the rock badgers are a people not mighty, yet they make their homes in the cliffs;
zh: 石貛並不是強壯的動物， 卻能在巖石中做窟。

en: the locusts have no king, yet all of them march in rank;
zh: 蝗蟲沒有君王， 卻能成群列隊出發。

en: the lizard you can take in your hands, yet it is in kings' palaces.
zh: 壁虎雖可以用手捕捉， 卻住在王宮中。

en: Three things are stately in their tread; four are stately in their stride:
zh: 步伐威武的東西有三樣， 連行走威武的，共有四樣：

en: the lion, which is mightiest among beasts and does not turn back before any;
zh: 就是在百獸中最威猛的獅子， 牠面對任何野獸，也不會退縮；

en: the strutting rooster, the he-goat, and a king whose army is with him.
zh: 還有高視闊步的 雄雞和公山羊， 以及率領軍兵的君王。

en: If you have been foolish, exalting yourself, or if you have been devising evil, put your hand on your mouth.
zh: 如果你行事愚昧，自高自大， 或是心懷惡計，就要用手掩口。

en: For pressing milk produces curds, pressing the nose produces blood, and pressing anger produces strife.
zh: 攪動牛乳必生出乳酪， 緊壓鼻子必壓出血來， 激起怒氣必產生紛爭。”
